forecast-period.text: A strong high pressure system in the Bight extending a ridge over northern SA, will continue to move eastwards to be centred over eastern SA late Wednesday and over VIC by Friday.
For latest warnings go to www.bom.gov.au, subscribe to RSS feeds, call 1300 659 210* or listen for warnings on relevant TV and radio broadcasts.
* Calls to 1300 numbers cost around 27.5c incl. GST, higher from mobiles or public phones.

Dry, apart from possible isolated morning showers about the Lower South East. Areas of fog about the agricultural areas and south of the pastoral districts. Maximum temperatures near average. Southeast to northeasterly winds.
Isolated showers about the agricultural area, clearing by evening. Southwest to southeasterly winds.

Dry. Areas of fog and frost about central and eastern parts of the agricultural areas and south of the pastoral districts. Maximum temperatures near average grading to below average in the far north. Southeast to northeasterly winds.

Dry. Areas of fog and frost about central and eastern parts of the agricultural areas and south of the pastoral districts. Maximum temperatures near average, tending above average in the west. North to northeasterly winds, southeast to northeasterly in the northeast.

text.p: Dry. Areas of fog and frost on and east of the ranges. Maximum temperatures slightly above average. North to northeasterly winds, southeast to northeasterly in the northeast.
Cumulative rainfall totals to the end of Saturday: Up to 2 mm about the southern agricultural area, mainly for the remainder of Tuesday.
